title: January 2023 CPI Report Shows 6.4% YoY Inflation
content: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported CPI-U increased 6.4% year-over-year in January 2023, slightly above consensus expectations of 6.2%.

title: March 2023 CPI Falls to 5.0% YoY
content: US CPI-U year-over-year inflation dropped to 5.0% in March 2023, the lowest reading since May 2021, driven by declining energy costs.

title: May 2023 CPI Drops to 4.0% YoY
content: CPI-U year-over-year inflation continued its decline to 4.0% in May 2023, suggesting June could fall below the 4% threshold.

title: Fed Raises Rates to 5.25-5.50% at July 2023 FOMC
content: The Federal Reserve raised the federal funds rate by 25 basis points to 5.25-5.50%, the highest level in 22 years.

title: Fed Seen Holding Rates Steady at September Meeting
content: Market expectations strongly favor a rate hold at the September FOMC meeting, with CME FedWatch showing 93% probability of no change.

title: August Jobs Report Supports Fed Pause in September
content: August nonfarm payrolls came in at 187,000, and prior months were revised down, reducing urgency for further rate hikes.

title: US Economy Shows Surprising Resilience in Mid-2023
content: Multiple indicators suggest the US economy is growing faster than expected, with strong consumer spending and a resilient labor market.

title: Q2 2023 GDP Revised Up to 2.1% Annualized
content: The Bureau of Economic Analysis revised Q2 2023 GDP growth to 2.1%, indicating continued economic momentum heading into Q3.

title: GDPNow Estimate for Q3 2023 Surges to 5.4%
content: The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model estimate for Q3 2023 GDP growth reached 5.4%, far exceeding Wall Street consensus.

title: July 2023 Unemployment Rate at 3.5%
content: US unemployment rate fell to 3.5% in July 2023, near historically low levels, with nonfarm payrolls adding 187,000 jobs.

title: October 2023 Unemployment Rate Ticks Up to 3.9%
content: The unemployment rate rose to 3.9% in October 2023, the highest in nearly two years but still well below 4.0%.

title: Fed Signals Higher for Longer at September Meeting
content: The FOMC dot plot showed fewer rate cuts expected in 2024, pushing Treasury yields sharply higher across the curve.

title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Breaches 5% for First Time Since 2007
content: The benchmark 10-year US Treasury yield topped 5% intraday for the first time since July 2007, driven by fiscal concerns and strong economic data.

title: ECB Raises Deposit Rate to 3.50%
content: The European Central Bank raised its deposit facility rate to 3.50%, signaling more hikes ahead as core inflation remains elevated.

title: ECB Raises Deposit Rate to 4.00% in Final Hike
content: The ECB raised its deposit rate to 4.00%, matching exactly but not exceeding the 4% threshold. The statement suggested this could be the terminal rate.

title: Core PCE Falls to 4.1% YoY in May 2023
content: The core PCE price index showed a year-over-year increase of 4.1% in May 2023, continuing a gradual disinflation trend.

title: September Core PCE Falls to 3.7% YoY
content: Core PCE inflation continued declining to 3.7% year-over-year in September, well below the 4% threshold and moving toward the Fed's target.

title: S&P 500 Enters Bull Market Territory Above 4300
content: The S&P 500 closed above 4,300 for the first time in 2023, driven by AI optimism and stronger-than-expected earnings.

title: S&P 500 Posts Best First Seven Months Since 1997
content: The S&P 500 closed at 4588 on July 31, having climbed more than 19% year-to-date, its best January-July performance in 26 years.

title: September 2023 Payrolls Surge by 336,000
content: US nonfarm payrolls far exceeded expectations with 336,000 jobs added in September, nearly double the consensus forecast.

title: November 2023 Payrolls Add 199,000 Jobs
content: Nonfarm payrolls added 199,000 jobs in November, slightly below expectations. October was revised to 150,000.

title: Dollar Index Strengthens on Resilient US Data
content: The DXY dollar index climbed to 105.3, supported by strong US economic data relative to weaker European and Chinese growth.

title: Dollar Weakens After Fed Signals Rate Cuts Ahead
content: The DXY fell to 102.1 after the Fed's December dot plot signaled three rate cuts in 2024, but remained above the 100 level.

title: China Q2 2023 GDP Growth at 6.3% YoY
content: China's economy grew 6.3% year-over-year in Q2 2023, boosted by a low base effect from 2022 lockdowns, but momentum was slowing.

title: China Economic Data Points to Full-Year Growth Near 5.2%
content: November industrial production and retail sales data suggest China is on track for approximately 5.2% GDP growth in 2023.

title: Weekly Jobless Claims Stay Below 250K
content: Initial jobless claims remained at 248,000 for the week ending July 1, staying well below the 300,000 threshold.

title: Jobless Claims Edge Down to 220K
content: Initial unemployment claims fell to 220,000, signaling continued labor market strength and no signs of approaching 300K.

title: BOJ Tweaks Yield Curve Control but Maintains Negative Rates
content: The Bank of Japan adjusted its yield curve control framework to allow 10-year yields to exceed 1%, but kept the short-term rate at -0.1%.

title: BOJ Governor Signals Possible End of Negative Rates in Spring
content: BOJ Governor Ueda indicated conditions are falling into place for an exit from negative interest rate policy, possibly at the March meeting.

title: Japan Spring Wage Negotiations Deliver Biggest Raises in 33 Years
content: Major Japanese companies agreed to wage increases averaging 5.28%, the strongest in 33 years, paving the way for BOJ policy normalization.

title: July Retail Sales Beat Expectations at 0.7% MoM
content: US advance retail sales rose 0.7% month-over-month in July 2023, indicating resilient consumer spending.

title: November Retail Sales Show Positive YoY Growth
content: November advance retail sales were 4.1% higher year-over-year, maintaining the streak of positive annual comparisons throughout H2 2023.

title: Fed Raises to 5.25-5.50% in July
content: The FOMC raised the fed funds rate to 5.25-5.50%, the 11th hike in the cycle. Markets expect this to be the final increase.

title: Fed Holds Rates Steady at 5.25-5.50% in December
content: The FOMC held rates at 5.25-5.50% for the third consecutive meeting. The dot plot now signals three 25bp cuts in 2024.

title: ISM Manufacturing PMI at 46.4 in July, 9th Straight Month of Contraction
content: The ISM Manufacturing PMI registered 46.4 in July, marking the ninth consecutive month below the 50 threshold.

title: September ISM Manufacturing PMI at 49.0, Still Below 50
content: The ISM Manufacturing PMI rose to 49.0 in September 2023 but remained below the 50 expansion threshold for all three months of Q3.

title: M2 Money Supply Continues Contraction
content: US M2 money supply fell 3.6% year-over-year in June 2023, the sharpest contraction since records began in the 1960s.

title: M2 Contraction Narrows to -2.3% YoY in November
content: M2 money supply was $20.74T in November 2023, still 2.3% below November 2022 levels, though the rate of decline is moderating.

title: Oil Prices Surge Past $93 on OPEC+ Supply Cuts
content: WTI crude oil prices jumped above $93/barrel as Saudi Arabia and Russia extended production cuts through year-end 2023.

title: Oil Prices Pull Back Below $85 Despite Middle East Tensions
content: WTI crude retreated to $84/barrel despite the Israel-Hamas conflict, as demand concerns offset geopolitical risk premium.

title: Euro Area GDP Revised Down to -0.1% in Q1 2023
content: Eurostat revised Q1 2023 Euro Area GDP growth to -0.1%, following a -0.1% reading in Q4 2022, meeting the technical recession definition.

title: Euro Area Q2 2023 GDP Growth Returns Positive at 0.1%
content: The Euro Area economy returned to slight growth of 0.1% in Q2 2023, ending the technical recession that lasted two quarters.

title: Euro Area Q4 2023 GDP Flat at 0.0%
content: The Euro Area economy stagnated with 0.0% growth in Q4 2023, avoiding a recession for the second half of the year.

title: August Trade Deficit Narrows to $58.3 Billion
content: The US trade deficit narrowed to $58.3 billion in August 2023, the smallest gap in three months, driven by strong exports.

title: November Trade Deficit Widens to $63.2 Billion
content: The US goods and services trade deficit widened to $63.2 billion in November, but multiple months in H2 had been below $60B.

title: McCarthy Ousted as Speaker in Historic Vote
content: Kevin McCarthy was removed as Speaker of the House in a historic vote, the first time in US history a speaker was ousted by their own party.

title: Jim Jordan Fails Third Vote for Speaker
content: Rep. Jim Jordan failed to secure enough votes on his third ballot for Speaker, with opposition growing among moderate Republicans.

title: Mike Johnson Elected Speaker of the House
content: Rep. Mike Johnson of Louisiana was elected Speaker of the House, ending three weeks of chaos and Republican infighting.

title: Congress Passes Stopgap Spending Bill to Avert Shutdown
content: The Senate passed a 45-day continuing resolution to fund the government through November 17, averting an October shutdown.

title: House Passes Clean CR Hours Before Shutdown Deadline
content: The House voted 335-91 to pass a clean continuing resolution, sending it to the Senate and averting a government shutdown.

title: Trump Leads Republican Primary Polls by 40+ Points
content: Donald Trump holds a commanding lead in the RealClearPolitics Republican primary average at 54.8%, more than 40 points ahead of DeSantis.

title: Trump Maintains 50+ Point Lead Ahead of Iowa Caucuses
content: With the Iowa caucuses days away, Trump leads the Republican primary field with 62.3% in the national RCP average.

title: No Major Economy Has Set Net-Zero 2040 Target
content: Analysis shows no country with GDP above $1 trillion has committed to a net-zero target earlier than 2050 as of mid-2023.

title: COP28 Ends Without New Accelerated Net-Zero Pledges
content: The COP28 climate summit concluded with an agreement to transition away from fossil fuels but no new net-zero-by-2040 commitments from major economies.

title: Ukraine's Counteroffensive Makes Slow Progress Near Bakhmut
content: Ukrainian forces have made incremental gains on the flanks of Bakhmut but have not entered the city center as of mid-August.

title: Ukrainian Operations Near Bakhmut Stall Amid Resource Shifts
content: Ukraine has shifted resources to the Zaporizhzhia axis, and offensive operations near Bakhmut have largely stalled with no significant city center progress.

title: EU Parliament Adopts Position on AI Act
content: The European Parliament adopted its negotiating position on the AI Act, setting the stage for trilogue negotiations with the Council.

title: EU Reaches Historic Agreement on AI Act After Marathon Talks
content: The European Parliament and Council reached a provisional agreement on the AI Act after 36 hours of marathon negotiations.

title: India Pushes for G20 Consensus Ahead of September Summit
content: Indian diplomatic officials are conducting intensive negotiations to bridge differences on Ukraine war language ahead of the New Delhi G20 summit.

title: G20 Leaders Adopt Joint Declaration in New Delhi
content: G20 leaders adopted a joint communique in New Delhi after India brokered a compromise on Ukraine-related language.

title: Milei Wins Argentina Primary in Surprise Result
content: Libertarian candidate Javier Milei won the most votes in Argentina's primary election, shocking pollsters and markets.

title: Milei Leads Polls Ahead of Argentine Runoff
content: Javier Milei holds a narrow lead in polls ahead of the November 19 presidential runoff against Sergio Massa.

title: FAA Administrator Nomination Faces Senate Delays
content: The confirmation process for FAA administrator nominee Phil Washington has stalled amid Republican opposition and safety concerns.

title: White House Nominates New FAA Chief After Washington Withdrawal
content: After Phil Washington withdrew, the White House nominated Michael Whitaker, but Senate confirmation is unlikely before year-end.

title: Poland Election Polls Show PiS Lead Narrowing
content: Recent polls show Poland's ruling PiS party with a narrow lead but short of a parliamentary majority, with Civic Coalition closing the gap.

title: PiS Wins Most Seats but Falls Short of Majority in Poland
content: PiS won the most seats in Poland's election but fell far short of a majority. The opposition led by Donald Tusk can form a coalition government.

title: Biden Administration Weighs New China Chip Export Controls
content: The Commerce Department is preparing additional restrictions on semiconductor exports to China, targeting advanced AI chips.

title: US Expands China Chip Export Controls
content: The Commerce Department announced expanded export controls on semiconductor equipment and AI chips to China, closing loopholes from 2022 rules.

title: South Africa Pushes for BRICS Expansion at Pre-Summit Meetings
content: South Africa, as BRICS chair for 2023, is actively pushing for the bloc to invite new members at the August summit in Johannesburg.

title: BRICS Invites Six New Members in Historic Expansion
content: BRICS leaders formally invited Argentina, Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, Saudi Arabia, and UAE to join the bloc starting January 2024.

title: Israeli Knesset Passes Reasonableness Clause Override
content: The Knesset passed a law limiting the Supreme Court's ability to use 'reasonableness' in judicial review, but the broader package remains incomplete.

title: Israel-Hamas War Halts Judicial Reform Efforts
content: The October 7 Hamas attack and subsequent war have effectively frozen all legislative efforts related to judicial reform in Israel.

title: Haley Rises to 8% in GOP Primary Polls
content: Nikki Haley has climbed to 8% in the RealClearPolitics Republican primary average, establishing herself in third place behind Trump and DeSantis.

title: Haley Surges to 14.5% Nationally but Remains Below 20%
content: Nikki Haley rose to 14.5% in the national RCP average after strong debate performances, but has not crossed the 20% threshold.

title: Biden Tightens AI Chip Export Controls to China
content: New executive order limits export of advanced AI semiconductors to China, adding restrictions on Nvidia and AMD high-end processors.

title: OpenAI DevDay Showcases GPT-4 Turbo but No GPT-5
content: OpenAI's first developer conference introduced GPT-4 Turbo and custom GPTs but made no mention of GPT-5 development or release timeline.

title: OpenAI CEO Altman Says GPT-5 Not Imminent
content: Sam Altman indicated that GPT-5 training is underway but the model is not expected to launch in the near term, focusing instead on GPT-4 improvements.

title: Apple Accelerates Mixed Reality Headset Development
content: Apple is in the final stages of developing its mixed-reality headset, with a reveal expected at WWDC in June 2023.

title: Apple Announces Vision Pro at WWDC 2023
content: Apple officially unveiled the Vision Pro mixed-reality headset at its Worldwide Developers Conference, priced at $3,499.

title: DeepMind's GNoME Discovers 2.2 Million New Crystal Structures
content: Google DeepMind published research on its GNoME model that predicted 2.2 million new crystal structures, a major advancement in materials science.

title: DeepMind's AlphaFold Update Extends Beyond Proteins
content: DeepMind published an update to AlphaFold that can predict structures of nucleic acids and small molecules with state-of-the-art accuracy.

title: Threads Launches and Gains 100 Million Users in 5 Days
content: Meta's Threads app launched on July 5 and quickly amassed 100 million sign-ups, but daily usage declined sharply in subsequent weeks.

title: X Retains US User Lead Over Threads, Bluesky
content: Sensor Tower data shows X (formerly Twitter) maintained approximately 60 million monthly active US users in Q4, well ahead of Threads at ~20M.

title: Waymo Begins Rides in Austin and Los Angeles
content: Waymo expanded its commercial robotaxi service to Austin, Texas and Los Angeles, California in 2023, beyond its original SF and Phoenix markets.

title: Cruise Suspends All Driverless Operations After Incidents
content: GM's Cruise suspended its driverless taxi service nationwide after California revoked its permit following safety incidents in San Francisco.

title: Neuralink Receives FDA Approval for Human Brain Implant Study
content: Neuralink announced it received FDA approval to conduct its first-in-human clinical trial for its brain-computer interface device.

title: Neuralink Begins Recruiting for Human Trials
content: Neuralink opened enrollment for its PRIME Study, seeking quadriplegic patients for the first human brain-computer interface implant.

title: GPT-4 Achieves 86.4% on MMLU Benchmark
content: OpenAI's GPT-4 scored 86.4% on the MMLU benchmark (5-shot), the highest score to date but below the 90% threshold.

title: Google's Gemini Ultra Scores 90.0% on MMLU
content: Google DeepMind announced Gemini Ultra achieved 90.0% on the MMLU benchmark (5-shot), the first model to surpass the 90% milestone.

title: US EV Sales Hit Record 300K in Q2 2023
content: Battery electric vehicle sales reached approximately 300,000 units in Q2 2023, putting full-year sales on pace to exceed 1 million.

title: US EV Sales Surpass 1.2 Million Units in 2023
content: Full-year 2023 US BEV sales reached approximately 1.19 million units, exceeding 1 million for the first time.

title: OpenAI Board Fires and Then Rehires Sam Altman as CEO
content: The chaotic five-day saga at OpenAI concluded with Sam Altman returning as CEO, with no indication of Claude 3 competitor release timeline.

title: Anthropic Expected to Release Claude 3 in Early March
content: Sources indicate Anthropic is preparing to launch Claude 3 in early March 2024, just after the question's close date.

title: Tesla Q2 2023 Deliveries: 466,140 Vehicles
content: Tesla delivered 466,140 vehicles in Q2 2023, a record quarter and ahead of analyst expectations, putting it on track for the annual target.

title: Tesla Delivers 1.81 Million Vehicles in 2023
content: Tesla reported full-year 2023 deliveries of 1,808,581 vehicles, narrowly exceeding the 1.8 million target.

title: First Republic Bank Seized and Sold to JPMorgan
content: First Republic Bank was seized by the FDIC and sold to JPMorgan Chase. First Republic was not in the top 10 US banks by assets at the time of failure.

title: US Banking System Stabilizes After Spring Turmoil
content: Major US banks report improved deposit flows and capital ratios, with no top-10 bank showing signs of distress.

title: AWS Revenue Reaches $22.1B in Q2 2023
content: Amazon Web Services generated $22.1 billion in net revenue in Q2 2023, a 12% year-over-year increase despite cloud optimization trends.

title: AWS Full-Year 2023 Revenue Tops $90.8 Billion
content: Amazon reported AWS net revenue of $90.8 billion for full-year 2023, exceeding the $90 billion milestone.

title: UK CMA Blocks Microsoft-Activision Deal
content: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority blocked Microsoft's $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard, citing cloud gaming concerns.

title: Microsoft Closes Activision Blizzard Acquisition After CMA Approval
content: Microsoft completed its $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard after restructuring cloud gaming rights to satisfy UK regulators.

title: Nvidia Forecasts Record Revenue, Market Cap Nears $1 Trillion
content: Nvidia's blockbuster Q2 revenue forecast of $11 billion sent shares soaring, pushing market cap to the cusp of $1 trillion.

title: Nvidia Becomes First Chipmaker With $1 Trillion Market Cap
content: Nvidia's market capitalization exceeded $1 trillion for the first time, driven by surging demand for AI training chips.

title: WeWork Warns of Going Concern, Shares Plunge
content: WeWork raised substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern in its quarterly filing, sending shares down 30%.

title: WeWork Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
content: WeWork filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, marking the end of the once $47 billion startup's tumultuous journey.

title: OpenAI Revenue Run Rate Exceeds $1 Billion
content: OpenAI's annualized revenue run rate has exceeded $1 billion, driven primarily by ChatGPT Plus subscriptions and API usage.

title: OpenAI Annual Revenue Approaches $1.6 Billion
content: OpenAI's revenue is reportedly approaching an annualized rate of $1.6 billion by end of 2023, well above the $1 billion milestone.

title: Big Tech Layoffs Slow Dramatically in H2 2023
content: After massive layoffs in early 2023, FAANG companies have significantly slowed workforce reductions, with most adding headcount in Q3.

title: No Major FAANG Layoff Announcements in H2 2023
content: None of the major FAANG companies announced layoffs exceeding 10,000 employees in the second half of 2023.

title: WGA Strike Enters Third Month with No Resolution in Sight
content: The Writers Guild of America strike, which began on May 2, has passed 60 days with no meaningful negotiations between the WGA and studios.

title: WGA Strike Ends After 148 Days with New Contract
content: The Writers Guild of America reached a tentative agreement with studios after 148 days on strike, well exceeding the 100-day mark.

title: Arm IPO Expected to Price at $47-51 Per Share
content: Arm Holdings is expected to price its IPO at $47-51 per share, implying a valuation of roughly $50-54 billion.

title: Arm Shares Surge 25% on First Day, Market Cap Exceeds $65 Billion
content: Arm Holdings shares jumped 25% on their Nasdaq debut, valuing the chip designer at approximately $65 billion.

title: Disney DTC Segment Loses $512M in Q3 FY2023
content: Disney's Direct-to-Consumer segment reported an operating loss of $512 million in the fiscal third quarter, narrower than the prior year.

title: Disney Streaming Losses Narrow to $387M in Q4 FY2023
content: Disney's streaming segment continued to reduce losses but remained unprofitable in fiscal Q4 2023 (July-September).

title: Global Semiconductor Sales Decline 21.6% YoY in April
content: The Semiconductor Industry Association reported global sales of $39.95 billion in April 2023, down 21.6% from April 2022.

title: 2023 Global Semiconductor Revenue Declined 8.2%
content: Full-year 2023 global semiconductor revenue fell 8.2% to $527 billion, confirming a year-over-year decline driven by memory chip weakness.

title: Boeing Delivers 43 737 MAX Aircraft in June 2023
content: Boeing delivered 43 737 MAX aircraft in June 2023, bringing its year-to-date MAX deliveries to approximately 225.

title: Boeing Delivers 396 737 MAX Aircraft in 2023
content: Boeing delivered 396 737 MAX aircraft in full-year 2023, falling well short of the 500-unit target amid ongoing supply chain constraints.

title: LK-99 Room Temperature Superconductor Claims Go Viral
content: Korean researchers claimed to have discovered a room-temperature ambient-pressure superconductor called LK-99, generating enormous interest worldwide.

title: LK-99 Replication Attempts Fail to Confirm Superconductivity
content: Multiple independent labs have failed to replicate the claimed superconducting properties of LK-99, with most results attributable to impurities.

title: June 2023 Breaks Global Temperature Record
content: June 2023 was the hottest June on record globally, with sea surface temperatures at unprecedented levels, per NOAA preliminary data.

title: 2023 Virtually Certain to Be Warmest Year on Record
content: Copernicus Climate Change Service data shows 2023 is virtually certain to be the warmest year in the instrumental record after the hottest October ever recorded.

title: NASA Confirms 2023 Was Hottest Year on Record
content: NASA's GISS analysis confirmed 2023 was the warmest year in the global temperature record, at 1.17C above the 20th century average.

title: WHO Reports Declining Mpox Cases Globally
content: The WHO reported a sustained decline in mpox cases worldwide, with weekly case counts dropping below 100 for the first time since the outbreak began.

title: WHO Declares End of Mpox Public Health Emergency
content: WHO Director-General Tedros declared the end of mpox as a public health emergency of international concern, though vigilance continues.

title: Moderna/Merck mRNA Cancer Vaccine Shows Promising Phase 2 Results
content: The mRNA-4157 personalized cancer vaccine combined with Keytruda reduced melanoma recurrence by 44% in Phase 2 trials.

title: Moderna/Merck Advance mRNA Cancer Vaccine to Phase 3
content: Moderna and Merck announced the initiation of a Phase 3 trial for their personalized mRNA cancer vaccine mRNA-4157/V940 in melanoma.

title: No New COVID Variants of Concern Designated by WHO in Q3
content: The WHO has not designated any new SARS-CoV-2 variants of concern since Omicron, though several sublineages are being monitored.

title: JN.1 Classified as Variant of Interest, Not Concern
content: The WHO classified the SARS-CoV-2 JN.1 sublineage as a separate variant of interest but did not elevate it to variant of concern status.

title: Vertex/CRISPR Sickle Cell Therapy Under FDA Review
content: The FDA accepted the Biologics License Application for exa-cel (Casgevy), a CRISPR-based therapy for sickle cell disease, with a December decision expected.

title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapies for Sickle Cell Disease
content: The FDA approved Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel), the first CRISPR-based gene therapy, for sickle cell disease in patients 12 and older.

title: OSIRIS-REx on Final Approach to Earth for Sample Delivery
content: NASA's OSIRIS-REx spacecraft is on track for its September 24 sample return, with the capsule expected to land in the Utah desert.

title: OSIRIS-REx Capsule Lands Successfully in Utah
content: NASA's OSIRIS-REx sample return capsule landed safely in the Utah desert, delivering approximately 250 grams of asteroid Bennu material.

title: March 2023 CO2 at Mauna Loa Reaches 421.0 ppm
content: NOAA reported Mauna Loa monthly mean CO2 concentration of 421.0 ppm for March 2023, continuing the upward trend.

title: May 2023 CO2 at Mauna Loa Peaks at 424.0 ppm
content: NOAA reported the May 2023 monthly mean CO2 at Mauna Loa was 424.0 ppm, setting a new seasonal record but below 425 ppm.

title: Chandrayaan-3 Successfully Launched from Sriharikota
content: ISRO launched Chandrayaan-3 aboard a LVM3 rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, beginning its journey to the lunar south pole.

title: Chandrayaan-3 Successfully Lands on Moon's South Pole
content: India's Chandrayaan-3 Vikram lander successfully touched down near the Moon's south pole, making India the fourth country to soft-land on the Moon.

title: NYC Building Permit Filings Up 12% in First Five Months
content: The NYC Department of Buildings reported a 12% increase in building permit filings through May 2023 compared to the same period in 2022.

title: NYC Issued 32,400 Building Permits in 2023
content: Full-year 2023 building permit data shows NYC DOB issued approximately 32,400 permits, exceeding the 30,000 threshold.

title: May Existing Home Sales Fall to 4.30 Million Annualized
content: Existing home sales dropped to a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 4.30 million in May, continuing the downtrend.

title: Existing Home Sales Drop to 3.96 Million in September
content: Existing home sales fell to a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 3.96 million in September 2023, the lowest pace since 2010.

title: US Median Existing Home Price Falls to $375,700 in March
content: The national median existing home price dropped to $375,700 in March 2023, well below the $400,000 threshold.

title: Median Existing Home Price Hits $410,200 in June
content: The national median existing home sale price reached $410,200 in June 2023, exceeding $400,000 on seasonal strength.

title: 30-Year Mortgage Rate Climbs to 7.09%
content: The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate rose to 7.09%, the highest since November 2022, dampening housing affordability.

title: Mortgage Rate Surges to 7.63%, Highest in 23 Years
content: The 30-year fixed mortgage rate hit 7.63% for the week of October 19, the highest level since November 2000.

title: Daily Mortgage Rate Briefly Touches 8.03%
content: The daily average 30-year fixed rate briefly exceeded 8% according to Mortgage News Daily, though the weekly Freddie Mac PMMS showed 7.79%.

title: Housing Starts Fall to 1.283 Million in August
content: US housing starts fell to a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 1.283 million in August 2023, well below the 1.5 million threshold.

title: December Housing Starts at 1.46 Million Annualized
content: Housing starts rose to 1.46 million annualized in December 2023, approaching but not exceeding the 1.5 million threshold in any month of H2.

title: Case-Shiller Index Shows First YoY Decline Since 2012
content: The S&P/Case-Shiller National Home Price Index fell 0.5% year-over-year in June 2023, the first annual decline since January 2012.

title: Case-Shiller National Index Rises 5.1% YoY in November
content: The S&P/Case-Shiller National Home Price Index showed a 5.1% year-over-year increase for November 2023, recovering strongly from mid-year declines.

title: NYC Rent Guidelines Board Considers 2-5% Increases
content: The NYC Rent Guidelines Board is weighing increases of 2-5% for one-year leases, with tenant advocates pushing for lower increases.

title: Rent Guidelines Board Approves 3% Increase for One-Year Leases
content: The NYC Rent Guidelines Board voted for a 3.0% increase for one-year rent-stabilized leases effective October 2023, exactly at the 3% threshold.

title: Manhattan Office Vacancy Rate Hits 16.1% in Q2 2023
content: CBRE reported Manhattan office vacancy reached 16.1% in Q2 2023, up from 14.8% a year earlier but still below 20%.

title: Manhattan Office Vacancy at 17.8% in Q4 2023
content: Manhattan office availability reached 17.8% in Q4 2023, elevated but remaining below the 20% threshold.

title: June New Home Sales Surge to 697K Annualized
content: New home sales jumped to a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 697,000 in June 2023, approaching but not exceeding 700K.

title: August New Home Sales Jump to 714K Annualized
content: New home sales surged to a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 714,000 in August 2023, exceeding 700K for the first time since early 2022.

title: California Housing Permits Pace Behind Annual Targets
content: Through May, California has approved approximately 38,000 residential unit permits, below the pace needed to reach 100,000 for the year.

title: California Approves 88,500 Housing Unit Permits in 2023
content: Full-year 2023 data shows California approved approximately 88,500 residential unit permits, falling short of the 100,000 target.

title: November Unemployment Rate Drops to 3.7%
content: The unemployment rate fell back to 3.7% in November 2023 with 199,000 jobs added, showing continued labor market resilience.

title: ECB Holds Deposit Rate at 4.00% in December
content: The ECB held its deposit facility rate at 4.00% in December, confirming the rate peaked at exactly 4.00% and did not exceed it.

title: October Payrolls Add 150,000 Jobs, Below 200K Threshold
content: Nonfarm payrolls increased by only 150,000 in October 2023, weighed down by the UAW strike, below the 200K question threshold.

title: China Reports 5.2% GDP Growth for 2023
content: China's National Bureau of Statistics reported the economy grew 5.2% in 2023, exceeding the government's roughly 5% target.

title: Fed Holds Rates at 5.25-5.50% in September
content: The FOMC held the fed funds rate unchanged at 5.25-5.50% in September while maintaining a hawkish bias on future rate path.

title: WTI Crude Settles at $93.68, Highest Since August 2022
content: WTI crude oil front-month futures settled at $93.68/barrel, the highest close since August 2022, on tightening OPEC+ supplies.

title: Trump Dominates GOP Primary Field at 58% in Polls
content: Donald Trump leads the RealClearPolitics Republican primary polling average at 58.1%, maintaining a 40+ point lead over all rivals.

title: EU AI Act Trilogue Negotiations Intensify Over Foundation Models
content: EU trilogue talks on the AI Act have become contentious over how to regulate foundation models and general-purpose AI systems.

title: US Adds More Chinese Entities to Export Control List
content: The US Commerce Department added additional Chinese semiconductor and AI companies to the Entity List, restricting technology exports.

title: Neuralink Implants Brain Chip in First Human Patient
content: Neuralink announced it had successfully implanted its brain-computer interface device in the first human patient as part of the PRIME Study.

title: Tesla Q3 2023 Deliveries: 435,059 Vehicles
content: Tesla delivered 435,059 vehicles in Q3 2023, bringing year-to-date deliveries to approximately 1,324,000, on pace for 1.8M full-year.

title: Microsoft Restructures Activision Deal for CMA Approval
content: Microsoft proposed restructuring the deal by divesting Activision's cloud gaming rights to Ubisoft to address UK CMA concerns.

title: OpenAI Revenue Accelerating on Enterprise Demand
content: OpenAI's monthly revenue has been accelerating throughout 2023, driven by growing enterprise API usage and ChatGPT Plus subscriptions.

title: WGA Strike Passes 100-Day Mark
content: The Writers Guild of America strike surpassed 100 days on August 10, becoming one of the longest writers' strikes in Hollywood history.

title: Global Chip Sales Show Signs of Recovery in July
content: Global semiconductor sales rose 2.3% month-over-month in July 2023, showing early signs of cyclical recovery though still down 11.8% YoY.

title: August 2023 Shatters Global Temperature Records
content: Copernicus Climate Change Service confirmed August 2023 was the hottest August on record by a large margin, with global temperatures 1.5C above pre-industrial levels.

title: FDA Advisory Committee Recommends Approval of CRISPR Sickle Cell Therapy
content: An FDA advisory committee unanimously recommended approval of Vertex/CRISPR Therapeutics' Casgevy for sickle cell disease.

title: December Existing Home Sales Fall to 3.78 Million Annualized
content: Existing home sales ended 2023 at a 3.78 million annualized rate in December, well below the 4 million threshold throughout much of the year.

title: Case-Shiller National Index Up 5.5% YoY in December 2023
content: The S&P/Case-Shiller National Home Price Index showed positive year-over-year growth of 5.5% for December 2023.

title: S&P 500 Ends Q3 at 4288 After Late-Quarter Selloff
content: The S&P 500 closed Q3 2023 at 4,288, having peaked at 4,588 in late July before a sharp selloff in August and September.

title: September Retail Sales Beat Expectations at 0.7% MoM
content: US advance retail sales rose 0.7% month-over-month in September 2023, significantly above the 0.3% consensus forecast.

title: M2 Money Supply Contraction Eases to -3.0% YoY in September
content: US M2 money supply contracted 3.0% year-over-year in September 2023, still negative but narrowing from the peak decline.

title: Argentina First Round: Massa Leads Milei by 7 Points
content: Economy Minister Sergio Massa surprised by winning the first round of Argentina's presidential election with 36.7% vs Milei's 30%.

title: DeepMind FunSearch Uses LLMs to Discover New Mathematical Solutions
content: DeepMind published FunSearch in Nature, using LLMs to discover new mathematical functions, marking a novel approach to AI-driven scientific discovery.

title: Q3 US EV Sales Hit 313,000 Units, New Record
content: US BEV sales reached approximately 313,000 in Q3 2023, bringing the year-to-date total to approximately 873,000 units.

title: Nvidia Reports Blowout Q1 and Forecasts $11B Q2 Revenue
content: Nvidia reported Q1 FY2024 revenue of $7.2 billion and guided Q2 to $11 billion, far exceeding the $7.2B consensus, driven by AI chip demand.

title: Disney Streaming Turns Near-Breakeven in Q1 FY2024
content: Disney's Direct-to-Consumer segment posted a small operating loss of $138M in Q1 FY2024 (Oct-Dec 2023), not yet reaching profitability.

title: Boeing 737 MAX Deliveries Reach 309 Through September
content: Boeing delivered 309 737 MAX aircraft through the first nine months of 2023, requiring approximately 191 more to reach 500 for the year.

title: Moderna Reports Strong Phase 3 Enrollment for mRNA Cancer Vaccine
content: Moderna and Merck announced rapid enrollment in the Phase 3 V940-001 trial testing the mRNA cancer vaccine in adjuvant melanoma treatment.

title: Chandrayaan-3 Successfully Enters Lunar Orbit
content: ISRO confirmed that Chandrayaan-3 has been successfully placed into lunar orbit, with the landing attempt scheduled for August 23.

title: Mortgage Rate Hits 7.76%, Nearing 8%
content: The 30-year fixed mortgage rate reached 7.76% in the weekly PMMS survey, approaching but not yet reaching the 8% mark in the official survey.

title: Manhattan Office Vacancy Edges to 17.2% in Q3 2023
content: Manhattan direct office vacancy rose to 17.2% in Q3 2023, as remote work patterns continue to weigh on demand.

title: Year-End Assessment: US Tightened China Chip Controls Multiple Times in 2023
content: The US government expanded semiconductor export controls to China in both October and December 2023, confirming new sanctions were imposed.

title: November Core PCE Falls to 3.2% YoY
content: The BEA reported core PCE inflation at 3.2% year-over-year for November 2023, well below 4% and continuing the disinflation trend.

title: Apple Reality Pro Headset in Advanced Prototype Stage
content: Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reports Apple's mixed-reality headset is in advanced prototype testing, with internal presentations to the board of directors.

title: FDIC Reports No Additional Top-10 Bank Failures in 2023
content: The FDIC's Q3 2023 Quarterly Banking Profile showed all top-10 US banks by assets remain well-capitalized with no distress indicators.

title: Waymo Announces Austin Commercial Launch
content: Waymo announced it will begin offering fully autonomous ride-hailing services in Austin, Texas, expanding beyond its initial SF and Phoenix markets.

title: July New Home Sales at 714K, Exceed 700K Threshold
content: New single-family home sales surged to an annualized rate of 714,000 in July 2023, the strongest pace in over a year.

title: California Permits Track Below 100K Annual Pace Through August
content: Year-to-date residential building permits in California total approximately 58,000 through August, well below the 100K annual pace.
